metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students explore housing design, interior design principles, housing systems, sustainable living practices, and architectural planning. This course develops skills for creating functional, aesthetically pleasing, and sustainable living environments.
metadata.general_description: Interior design, housing systems, sustainable living, and architectural planning for residential environments.

big_ideas.statement: Housing provides shelter while expressing personal identity, reflecting cultural values, and supporting quality of life
big_ideas.elaboration: Homes are more than physical structures; they are personal spaces reflecting values, traditions, and aspirations.

big_ideas.statement: Interior design combines aesthetics, function, and human psychology to create purposeful living spaces
big_ideas.elaboration: Effective interior design considers how design elements affect mood, productivity, safety, and daily living patterns.

big_ideas.statement: Building systems and construction integrate structural, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing components
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding how systems work together ensures homes are safe, efficient, comfortable, and maintainable.

big_ideas.statement: Sustainable housing reduces environmental impact while providing comfort, health, and affordability
big_ideas.elaboration: Sustainable design addresses energy efficiency, water conservation, material selection, indoor air quality, and accessibility.

competencies.raw: Design interior spaces that combine aesthetics, function, and comfort
i_can_statements: I can apply design principles: balance, proportion, emphasis, variety
I can select color schemes and create mood
I can plan furniture arrangement and space flow
I can design spaces for different purposes and users

competencies.raw: Design residential spaces that accommodate diverse needs and lifestyles
i_can_statements: I can assess user needs and preferences
I can design accessible and inclusive spaces
I can plan flexible spaces for changing needs
I can incorporate personal expression and cultural elements

competencies.raw: Design sustainable and energy-efficient housing solutions
i_can_statements: I can select sustainable building materials
I can plan energy-efficient systems and features
I can design water-efficient features
I can incorporate healthy indoor environments

competencies.raw: Design complete housing solutions considering site, systems, and livability
i_can_statements: I can analyze sites for housing development
I can plan building layouts and flows
I can integrate mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems
I can design outdoor living spaces

competencies.raw: Apply interior design principles to plan and execute designs
i_can_statements: I can create interior design plans and presentations
I can develop color palettes and material selections
I can create mood boards and design concepts
I can evaluate and refine designs

competencies.raw: Apply knowledge of housing systems to understand building functionality
i_can_statements: I can identify structural systems and components
I can understand mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems
I can assess energy efficiency and performance
I can identify maintenance and repair needs

competencies.raw: Apply architectural and construction knowledge to design practical spaces
i_can_statements: I can read and create architectural drawings and plans
I can use appropriate scale and measurements
I can apply building codes and design standards
I can estimate costs and materials

competencies.raw: Apply sustainable design principles to reduce environmental impact
i_can_statements: I can calculate and reduce energy consumption
I can minimize water use and waste
I can select and source sustainable materials
I can improve indoor environmental quality

competencies.raw: Use computer-aided design and visualization software
i_can_statements: I can use CAD software to create floor plans and elevations
I can use 3D modeling and visualization tools
I can create perspective drawings and renderings
I can navigate and manipulate digital designs

competencies.raw: Use design software for concept development and presentation
i_can_statements: I can use design software to create mood boards
I can develop presentations for design concepts
I can create color and material studies
I can prepare professional design documentation

competencies.raw: Use technology to research materials, systems, and best practices
i_can_statements: I can research building materials and sustainability
I can investigate energy-efficient systems
I can analyze housing trends and innovations
I can evaluate products and manufacturers

competencies.raw: Use technology to simulate and analyze building performance
i_can_statements: I can use energy simulation software
I can model natural lighting and ventilation
I can calculate space requirements and efficiency
I can evaluate environmental performance

areas.name: Interior Design Principles and Applications
topics: Design elements: line, shape, color, texture, light
Design principles: balance, proportion, emphasis, unity, variety
Psychology of design and human factors
